variant: quick heads up: i'm vegetarian and i work out at 6am before work

request.message: quick heads up: i'm vegetarian and i work out at 6am before work

response_body.response:
got it. vegetarian, 6am workouts. that's locked in. when you're ready to build out your fitmax or any other plan, i'll anchor everything around that early window and keep it plant-based.

anything you want to tackle first, or just letting me know the setup?

variant: what should i eat after my workout?

request.message: what should i eat after my workout?

response_body.response:
post-workout you want protein + carbs within an hour or two of finishing. since you're wrapping at around 7am, aim to eat by 8am max. for a vegetarian post-workout meal at that time, go with:
- **protein**: greek yogurt, cottage cheese, eggs, or a scoop of whey/pea protein powder mixed into oats
- **carbs**: oats, rice, potatoes, fruit, or toast example: greek yogurt with granola and berries, or scrambled eggs with toast and a banana. the carbs refill your glycogen and drive the pump; the protein kicks recovery. keep it simple and quick since you're heading to work. doesn't need to be a full meal, just enough to hit 30g+ protein and some carbs.

products.name: Optimum Nutrition Gold Standard 100% Whey Protein
products.description: 24g whey isolate per scoop — third-party tested, low-cost per gram.

variant: and when should i eat it?

request.message: and when should i eat it?

response_body.response:
right, you're good — all vegetarian options. greek yogurt, cottage cheese, eggs, oats, fruit, toast work great.

timing: eat within 30–60 minutes post-workout if you can. your window closes around 7–8am before work, so aim to have something down by then. if you're cutting it close, a protein shake (whey or pea powder with banana and oats) takes 2 minutes and hits the same mark.
